content.claim: Small-school retention in rural Hungary has historically reduced between-school segregation by limiting parallel classes, creating a large supply of single-class-per-grade schools in small settlements; however, whether consolidation would reduce or increase segregation is contested, as non-Roma families may shift to church-run schools perceived as having greater admissions discretion.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Direction of segregation effect (reduction vs. increase post-consolidation) is actively disputed among cited sources
No ex-ante segregation-impact modeling or simulation of consolidation scenarios found
Admissions discretion in church schools is reported anecdotally, not quantified
Causality between school closure and non-Roma exit is inferred, not empirically demonstrated in cited sources
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
